*** 1 INJ *** Update to time of previous tornado report: A National Weather Service survey team, with additional help from spotter video footage, concluded a tornado occurred in far northeast Burnet County during the late afternoon of May 1st. The tornado formed just west of County Road 223, about 1/10 of a mile from the Burnet/Lampasas County line. The tornado continued south along County Road 223 just west of the Lampasas River for a short distance, then turned southwest crossing Panther Creek Road. After this, and based on chaser accounts, the tornado may have briefly lifted and reformed. The tornado then turned southeast following along and just east of County Road 223. The tornado is estimated to have dissipated near Taylor Lane. Due to the nearly continuous damage path this is listed as one tornado. Along the tornado path significant damage was observed, include trunks of trees snapped and damage to homes and outbuildings. Based on damage the peak wind of the tornado was estimated to be up to 110 mph (EF-1) around Panther Creek Road to the 4000-5000 block of County Road 223. One minor injury was reported to the driver of a delivery truck that was flipped on its side.

*** 1 INJ *** NWS survey team, with additional help from spotter video footage, has concluded a tornado occurred in far northeast Burnet County during the late afternoon of May 1st. The tornado formed just west of County Road 223, about 1/10 of a mile from the Burnet/Lampasas County line. The tornado continued south along County Road 223 and the Lampasas River for a short distance, then turned southwest crossing Panther Creek Road. Shortly after the tornado turned southeast following along and just east of County Road 223. The tornado is estimated to have dissipated just east of Taylor Lane. Along the tornado path significant damage was observed, include trunks of trees snapped and damage to homes and outbuildings. Based on damage the peak wind of the tornado was estimated to be up to 110 mph (EF-1) around Panther Creek Road to the 4000-5000 block of County Road 223. One minor injury was reported to the driver of a delivery truck that was flipped on its side.
*** 1 INJ *** Report of 5 homes damaged along CR 223. 1 driver was injured on CR 223. NWS will be surveying damage on Friday.

Southwest Hays County Tornado 1 rated EF0. The survey team determined that this tornado formed just east of Purgatory Road, south of Farm to Market Road 32. The most significant damage was seen along Cielo Ranch Road, with tree damage and minor damage to structures.
Damage to trees, windows, and roof reported. Tornadic debris signature observed on radar.

Southwest Hays County Tornado 2 rated EF0. The track of the second tornado was estimated through a combination of information gathered by the survey team and through radar. While much of the damage fell to the north of northeast, indications are that the tornado moved just north of the intersection of Teton Circle and Appalachian Road, with the most significant damage occurring in this area.

*** 1 FATAL *** Training of thunderstorms with intense rainfall rates occurred across the region. A swath of 3-8 inches of rain was measured. *** 10 FATAL, 4 INJ *** Corrects previous flash flood report from 2 WSW Windcrest. Updated fatality count from previous flash flood report. Training of thunderstorms with intense rainfall rates occurred across the region. A swath of 3-8 inches of rain was measured. At least 15 vehicles were swept into Beitel Creek near Loop 410 and Perrin Beitel Road area. There are ten confirmed fatalities in this creek and/or across San Antonio and unknown number of unaccounted for at the time of this new update. Ten people were rescued and two others were able to get to safety on their own.

*** 11 FATAL, 4 INJ *** Corrects previous flash flood report from 2 WSW Windcrest. Updated fatality count from previous flash flood report. Training of thunderstorms with intense rainfall rates occurred across the region. A swath of 3-8 inches of rain was measured. At least 15 vehicles were swept into Beitel Creek near Loop 410 and Perrin Beitel Road area. There are at least 11 confirmed fatalities in this creek and/or in nearby portions of San Antonio and unknown number of unaccounted for at the time of this new update. Ten people were rescued and two others were able to get to safety on their own.

*** 11 FATAL, 4 INJ *** Corrects previous flash flood report from 2 WSW Windcrest. Updated report as all missing persons are now accounted for. Training of thunderstorms with intense rainfall rates occurred across the region. A swath of 3-8 inches of rain was measured. At least 15 vehicles were swept into Beitel Creek near Loop 410 and Perrin Beitel Road area. There are 11 confirmed fatalities in this creek. Ten people were rescued and two others were able to get to safety on their own.

*** 4 FATAL *** Training of thunderstorms with intense rainfall rates occurred across the region. A swath of 3-8 inches of rain was measured. Several vehicles were swept off the Loop 410 access road into Beitel Creek. There are four confirmed fatalities in this creek and at least two other missing. Several other vehicles remain submerged at the time of this writing. Ten people were rescued in this creek and two others were able to get to safety on their own.

*** 4 FATAL *** Training of thunderstorms with intense rainfall rates occurred across the region. A swath of 3-8 inches of rain was measured. Several vehicles were swept off the Loop 410 access road with a total of 13 vehicles ending up in Beitel Creek. There are four confirmed fatalities in this creek and at least two other missing. Ten people were rescued in this creek and two others were able to get to safety on their own.

*** 5 FATAL, 4 INJ *** Updated fatality, injury, and unaccounted for count from previous flash flood report. Training of thunderstorms with intense rainfall rates occurred across the region. A swath of 3-8 inches of rain was measured. At least 15 vehicles were swept into Beitel Creek near Loop 410 and Perrin Beitel Road area. There are five confirmed fatalities in this creek and three unaccounted for at the time of this update. Ten people were rescued and two others were able to get to safety on their own.

*** 9 FATAL, 4 INJ *** Corrects previous flash flood report from 2 WSW Windcrest. Updated fatality count from previous flash flood report. Training of thunderstorms with intense rainfall rates occurred across the region. A swath of 3-8 inches of rain was measured. At least 15 vehicles were swept into Beitel Creek near Loop 410 and Perrin Beitel Road area. There are nine confirmed fatalities in this creek and unknown number of unaccounted for at the time of this new update. Ten people were rescued and two others were able to get to safety on their own.

An NWS storm survey team concluded a tornado caused EF-1 damage from 4 SW of Kendalia in Kendall County to 3 SSE of Fischer in Comal County. There was damage along a 20 mile track. The worst was knocked down power and light poles. Strongest winds were estimated to be 110 mph.
